Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver, If Any To Make Objections
Off Calendar Jul-31-2006 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Pacific Contruction'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One From Mst Constructors, Inc. And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Deidree Y. M. K. Sakai for plaintiff and moving party Pacific Construction
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Ronald K. Brown for defendant/Cross complainant and opposing party MST Construction

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Pacific Construction's motion to compel further responses to request for production is granted in part. All objections, other than based on the attorney client and attorney work product privilege are overruled and stricken. The general objections are stricken. There is no provision in the Code of Civil Procedure for general objections. Counsel for defendant is admonished to refrain from asserting such objections in the future. If any documents are withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log and served on plaintiff within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that counsel for defendant failed to respond in a timely manner, asserted unmeritorious objections, and failed to comply with CCP 2031.270. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1790 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
